seo.title: Building Customer Support with BeeAI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build customer support with BeeAI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building customer support agents with BeeAI enables scalable, memory-aware AI interactions with CRM integrations and RAG capabilities. However, long sessions risk unbounded memory growth, tool injection vulnerabilities, and inconsistent model outputs that can lead to inaccurate responses or policy violations. Vex provides essential guardrails to ensure reliability, PII safety, and compliance in high-risk support scenarios.

architectureOverview.heading: Customer Support Architecture with BeeAI
architectureOverview.description:
The BeeAI customer support agent follows a RAG architecture with vector search over a knowledge base for product info and FAQs, integrated with CRM APIs for ticket creation and order lookups. Agent memory persists conversation history across sessions, while tools handle actions like querying customer orders or escalating tickets. Observability events capture agent state changes, tool calls, and LLM outputs for monitoring.

Key components include: an LLM (Granite) for reasoning, a vector store for retrieval, BeeAI's Agent class for orchestration, and event listeners for logging. Sessions maintain state via BeeAI's memory manager, with tools validated before execution to prevent errors.

This setup supports multi-turn conversations but exposes risks like memory bloat in extended support threads or serialization failures during state persistence.

risks.name: Unbounded Agent Memory Growth
risks.description: Long support sessions accumulate conversation history without pruning, causing OOM errors and slow responses.
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors memory usage and enforces summarization triggers.

risks.name: Tool Injection via Invalid Inputs
risks.description: User prompts bypass tool validation, enabling arbitrary API calls to CRM systems.
risks.mitigation: Vex input sanitization and semantic validation before tool execution.

risks.name: PII Leakage in Responses
risks.description: Agent retrieves and echoes customer data (SSN, addresses) without redaction.
risks.mitigation: Vex PII entity extraction and filtering on all outputs.

risks.name: Granite Output Format Drift
risks.description: Model inconsistencies break JSON tool calls or structured responses.
risks.mitigation: Vex schema enforcement and retry logic on parse failures.

implementation.title: Initialize BeeAI Agent with Memory and Tools
implementation.description: Set up the core agent with persistent memory and customer support tools.
implementation.code:
from beeai import Agent, Memory
from beeai.tools import Tool

memory = Memory(persist=True)
agent = Agent(
    model='ibm/granite-13b',
    memory=memory,
    tools=[
        Tool(
            name='create_ticket',
            description='Create support ticket',
            func=lambda **kwargs: print(f'Ticket: {kwargs}')
        ),
        Tool(
            name='lookup_order',
            description='Lookup customer order',
            func=lambda order_id: f'Order {order_id}: Status shipped'
        )
    ]
)

implementation.title: Add RAG Knowledge Base Retrieval
implementation.description: Integrate vector search for product knowledge and FAQs.
implementation.code:
from beeai.retrievers import VectorRetriever

retriever = VectorRetriever.from_documents(
    docs=kb_docs,  # Pre-loaded support KB
    embedding_model='sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2'
)
agent.add_retriever(retriever)

# Usage in session
response = agent.run('How do I reset my password?', retriever=True)

implementation.title: Handle Multi-Turn Support Sessions
implementation.description: Process customer queries with CRM context preservation.
implementation.code:
session = agent.create_session(user_id='cust_123')

# Multi-turn interaction
session.update('I need help with order #456')
response1 = session.run()
print(response1)

session.update('Can you create a ticket?')
response2 = session.run()
print(response2)

# Persist state
session.save()

implementation.title: Add Event Listeners for Observability
implementation.description: Register callbacks for agent events to enable Vex integration.
implementation.code:
def on_tool_call(event):
    print(f'Tool called: {event.tool_name}')

def on_memory_update(event):
    print(f'Memory size: {len(event.memory)}')

agent.add_event_listener('tool_call', on_tool_call)
agent.add_event_listener('memory_update', on_memory_update)
agent.add_event_listener('llm_output', lambda e: print(e.output))

vexIntegration.description: Hook Vex SDK into agent events for real-time monitoring.
